senses.gloss: to go the right way, to follow the right course
to hold the right belief, to be orthodox
to be well guided
to be of age
to cause to follow the right course, to lead on the right course
to guide, to preserve from error
to cause to hold a right belief, to cause to be orthodox
to pronounce (someone) to be one who follows the right course (said of a judge)
to pronounce (someone) to be orthodox (said of a judge)
verbal noun of رَشَدَ (rašada) (form I)
going the right way, following the right course
holding the right belief, being orthodox
being well guided
being of age, maturity of intellect
rectitude, right conduct combined with firmness
verbal noun of رَشِدَ (rašida) (form I)
going the right way, following the right course
holding the right belief, being orthodox
being well guided
being of age, maturity of intellect
rectitude, right conduct combined with firmness
right path

body.text: ; (S, A, L, Msb, K;) the former of which is the better known and the more chaste; (TA;) inf. n.
, (S, L, Msb, K,) which is of the former, (S, L,) and
, (S, L, Msb, K,) which is of the latter, (S, L, Msb,) and
, (L, K,) which is also of the latter verb, (TA,) or this last is a simple subst.; (Msb;)
(S, A, L, Msb, K;) as to a road, and also as to an affair: (L:) [and often relating to religion; meaning
he held a right belief; was orthodox:
signifies the same: (L, K:) you say,
followed, a right way to conduct his affair:
followed, a right course in his affair;
this latter being a phrase similar to
relates to the things of the present life and to those of the life to come; and
, only to those of the life to come: but this distinction does not accord with what has been heard from the Arabs, nor with readings of the Kur-án, in which some read
in several verses. (MF.) The former also signifies The
continuing in the way of truth,
the right way, with self-constraining firmness in so doing.
